popularStory.headline: en.Wikipedia Frances Brandon, Duchess of Suffolk
popularStory.description: Frances Grey, Duchess of Suffolk (née Lady Frances Brandon; 16 July 1517 – 20 November 1559), was an English noblewoman, the second child and eldest daughter of King Henry VIII's younger sister, Princ …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Frances Brandon於1517年7月16日出生在英國賀福爾哈特菲爾德時，她的父親Charles Brandon 1st Duke of Suffolk33歲，母親Mary Tudor21歲。她於1533年與Henry Grey 1st Duke of Suffolk在英國素瑞結婚。他們至少是4個女兒的父母。她去世於1559年11月21日在英國米德萊可維斯特明斯特維斯特明斯特 艾比享年42歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭倫敦維斯特明斯特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Frances Brandon于1517年7月16日出生在英国賀福爾哈特菲爾德时，她的父亲Charles Brandon 1st Duke of Suffolk33岁，母亲Mary Tudor21岁。她于1533年与Henry Grey 1st Duke of Suffolk在英国素瑞结婚。他们至少是4个女儿的父母。她去世于1559年11月21日在英国米德萊可維斯特明斯特維斯特明斯特 艾比享年42岁，埋葬在英国英格兰倫敦維斯特明斯特。

nameMeanings.name: Brandon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Brandon, in Durham, Norfolk, Suffolk, Warwickshire, and elsewhere. Most are named with Old English brōm ‘broom, gorse’ + dūn ‘hill’. One in Lincolnshire, however, may be named after the Brant River, on which it stands; Ekwall derives the river name from Old English brant ‘steep’, presumably with reference to its steep banks. This surname was known in Ireland early enough to have a Gaelicized form de Breandún.
Irish (Kerry): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Breandáin ‘son of Breandán’, a rare Kerry surname from the Latinized form of the name of the local saint, Brendan (Brendanus from Bréanainn).
French: from the Old French oblique case of the personal name Brando, of ancient Germanic origin (see Brand 1).

variants.name: Brand
meanings: English, Scottish, North German, Scandinavian, Dutch, and French (mainly Alsace and Lorraine): from the ancient Germanic personal name Brando, Old Norse Brandr, a short form of various compound names containing the element brand ‘sword’ (a derivative of brinnan ‘to flash’), of which the best known is Hildebrand . There is placename evidence for Brant(a) as an Old English personal name; however, the Middle English personal name Brand was probably introduced to England from Old Norse.
German: variant of   Brandt   1 and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.
Swedish and Danish: ornamental name or nickname from brand ‘fire’.

variants.name: Brandom
meanings: English and Jewish (Sephardic): variant of   Brandon  .

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘tendril’ or ‘creeper’, often used in metaphors describing the slender curvature of eyebrows, arms, hair, swords, lightning, and women's bodies.

nameMeanings.name: Frances
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Feminine form of   Francis .  In the 16th century the two spellings were used indiscriminately for both sexes, the distinction in spelling not being established until the 17th century.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Frances Brandon was born on 16 July 1517, in Hatfield, Herefordshire, England, her father, Charles Brandon 1st Duke of Suffolk, was 33 and her mother, Mary Tudor, was 21. She married Henry Grey 1st Duke of Suffolk in 1533, in Surrey, England. They were the parents of at least 4 daughters. She died on 21 November 1559, in Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, England, at the age of 42, and was buried in Westminster, London, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Frances Brandon est née le 16 juillet 1517, à Hatfield, Herefordshire, Angleterre, son père, Charles Brandon 1st Duke of Suffolk, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Mary Tudor, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé Henry Grey 1st Duke of Suffolk en 1533, à Surrey, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 21 novembre 1559, à Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 42 ans, et a été enterrée à Westminster, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Frances Brandon am 16. Juli 1517 in Hatfield, Herefordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Charles Brandon 1st Duke of Suffolk 33 und ihre Mutter Mary Tudor 21. Sie hat 1533 in Surrey, England Henry Grey 1st Duke of Suffolk geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 21. November 1559 im Alter von 42 Jahren in Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, England und wurde in Westminster, London, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Frances Brandon è nata il il 16 luglio 1517 nel Hatfield, Herefordshire, England, sua padre, Charles Brandon 1st Duke of Suffolk, aveva 33 anni e sua madre, Mary Tudor, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato Henry Grey 1st Duke of Suffolk nel 1533, nel Surrey, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figlie. Morì il 21 novembre 1559, nel Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, England, all'età di 42 anni, e fu sepolta a Westminster, London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Frances Brandonが1517年7月16日にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアハトフィールドで生まれた時，父Charles Brandon 1st Duke of Suffolkは33歳，母Mary Tudorは21歳であった。1533年にイギリススリーでHenry Grey 1st Duke of Suffolkと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1559年11月21日にイギリスミッドレセックスウエストミンスターウエストミンスター アビーで42歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスロンドンウエストミンスターにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Frances Brandon(이)가 1517년 7월 16일 영국 허퍼드실르 하트필드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Charles Brandon 1st Duke of Suffolk(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mary Tudor(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1533년 영국 수리에서 Henry Grey 1st Duke of Suffolk(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1559년 11월 21일 영국 미들레세스 웨스트민스터 웨스트민스터 애브브이에서 42세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 런던 웨스트민스터에 묻혔다.

pt.16 July 1517–21 November 1559: 16 de julho de 1517–21 de novembro de 1559
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Frances Brandon nasceu em 16 de julho de 1517, em Hatfield, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Charles Brandon 1st Duke of Suffolk, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Mary Tudor, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com Henry Grey 1st Duke of Suffolk em 1533, em Surrey, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 21 de novembro de 1559, em Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra, com 42 anos, e foi sepultada em Westminster, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Frances Brandon родилась 16 июля 1517 г. в местности хатфиелд, хирефордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Charles Brandon 1st Duke of Suffolk было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Mary Tudor, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henry Grey 1st Duke of Suffolk в 1533 г. в местности сурри, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 дочери. Она умерла 21 ноября 1559 г. в местности вестминстир эбби, вестминстир, миддлесекс, Англия в возрасте 42 лет и была похоронена в местности вестминстир, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания.

es.16 July 1517–21 November 1559: 16 de julio de 1517–21 de noviembre de 1559
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Frances Brandon nació el 16 de julio de 1517, en Hatfield, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Charles Brandon 1st Duke of Suffolk, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Mary Tudor, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Henry Grey 1st Duke of Suffolk en 1533, en Surrey, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijas. Murió el 21 de noviembre de 1559, en Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 42 años, y fue sepultada en Westminster, London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
